title: Fitbit LLC — Consumer harm
investigation_objective: The Federal Court ordered US-based Fitbit LLC to pay an $11 million penalty after it admitted making false or misleading representations to 58 Australian consumers about their statutory consumer guarantee rights - telling some consumers a refund was only available if a faulty device was returned within 45 days, and telling others no replacement was available once the device's two-year warranty period had expired, both contrary to consumer guarantees under the Australian Consumer Law that are not time-limited in this way.
scope: Admitted making false or misleading representations to 58 Australian consumers about consumer guarantee rights; Ordered by the Federal Court to pay an $11 million penalty; Alleged to have imposed a false 45-day return window and a false post-warranty replacement bar contrary to statutory consumer guarantees
